scored_hypotheses.claim: The text uses a simple substitution cipher on medieval Latin, which would produce glyph entropy ~4.0

test_result.test: claude_evaluation
test_result.details: Glyph entropy (3.86 bits) nearly matches expected ~4.0 bits, supporting the hypothesis, but the Zipf exponent (0.8946) and extremely high hapax ratio (0.701) are inconsistent with a simple substitution cipher on medieval Latin, suggesting either a more complex encryption or anomalous language properties.

test_result.details: The 70.1% hapax ratio and 3.86 bits glyph entropy indicate topic fragmentation and diverse vocabulary, contradicting single-narrative coherence that PageRank would reveal as disconnected clusters.
